title: Rich Creamy Thai Soy Milk Khanom Krok
description: A nutty and creamy Thai dish featuring soy milk and ferment-jang, perfect for a sick day, this plant-based friendly khanom krok is a delicious and comforting treat. It is made with a combination of soy milk, rice flour, and coconut milk, giving it a rich and creamy texture.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together the soy milk, rice flour, and palm sugar until smooth.
Add the coconut milk, ferment-jang, and salt to the bowl and whisk until well combined.
Heat the coconut oil in a non-stick pan over medium heat.
Using a spoon, drop small amounts of the batter into the pan and cook until the bottoms are lightly browned.
Loosen the khanom krok with a spatula and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Serve the khanom krok warm and enjoy.
Repeat the cooking process until all of the batter is used up.
Let the khanom krok cool on a wire rack before serving.
